assertions.description: document.visibilityState is the spec-defined signal that flips before a page is frozen — Chrome 139 uses this same lifecycle just with a tighter Android timer.
document.hidden is exposed as the boolean shortcut for visibilityState !== 'visible'.
document.addEventListener('visibilitychange', ...) is accepted; this is the event that fires before background freezing.
Page Lifecycle 'freeze'/'resume' events are observable on document — these are the events delivered when Android backgrounds the tab and freezes it (faster in v139).
PageTransitionEvent is exposed (used for pageshow/pagehide which fire around freeze/resume).
If the visitor is on Android, the UA contains 'Android' (the platform the faster freezing actually applies to).
